package_metadata.title: Override Aws S3 Lifecycle Same Day Precedence
package_metadata.license: CC-BY-4.0 for benchmark adaptation; original source remains under its own terms.

provenance.adaptation_method: Paraphrased and condensed into a synthetic scenario while preserving the relation pattern needed for benchmark evaluation. The case is not a substitute for the source document.
provenance.professional_guidance_warning: Evaluation artifact only; do not use as legal, tax, clinical, cloud operations, immigration, or institutional policy advice.

source_excerpts.text:
S3 lifecycle note.

Section A. Lifecycle actions. A bucket can have multiple lifecycle rules that make an object eligible for actions such as transition to another storage class, expiration, or creation of a delete marker.

Section B. Same-day precedence. If multiple lifecycle rules make the same object eligible for more than one action on the same day, S3 follows precedence rules: permanent deletion takes precedence over transition, and transition takes precedence over creating a delete marker.

S3 lifecycle note.

<rule id="R1">Section A. Lifecycle actions. A bucket can have multiple lifecycle rules that make an object eligible for actions such as transition to another storage class, expiration, or creation of a delete marker.</rule>

<override id="O1" applies_to="R1" condition="same object eligible for multiple lifecycle actions on same day">Section B. Same-day precedence. If multiple lifecycle rules make the same object eligible for more than one action on the same day, S3 follows precedence rules: permanent deletion takes precedence over transition, and transition takes precedence over creating a delete marker.</override>

compiled_representation.content:
rules:
  - id: R1
    lifecycle_actions: [transition, expiration, create_delete_marker, permanent_deletion]
overrides:
  - id: O1
    trigger: same_object_same_day_multiple_actions
    precedence_order:
      - permanent_deletion
      - transition
      - create_delete_marker

rules.description: Adapted from AWS S3 Lifecycle precedence rules. Level-2 override: same-day lifecycle actions have fixed precedence.

decision_rules.query: An S3 object becomes eligible today for both permanent deletion and transition to another storage class under two lifecycle rules. Which lifecycle action takes precedence?
decision_rules.expected_decision: Permanent deletion takes precedence over transition.
decision_rules.rationale: Permanent deletion takes precedence. The general availability of transition does not control when the same object is also eligible for permanent deletion on the same day.

qa_pairs.question: An S3 object becomes eligible today for both permanent deletion and transition to another storage class under two lifecycle rules. Which lifecycle action takes precedence?
qa_pairs.answer: Permanent deletion takes precedence. The general availability of transition does not control when the same object is also eligible for permanent deletion on the same day.
Actions such as transition, expiration, delete marker creation, or permanent deletion.
Permanent deletion takes precedence over transition.

composition_hallucination: Transition it first because lifecycle transitions are configured for the object.
Create a delete marker because expiration is one of the rules.
wrong_escalation: Disable all lifecycle rules to avoid conflict.
failure_modes.retrieval_failure_note: If any necessary fragment is absent from context, classify the run as retrieval failure rather than composition hallucination.

controls.negative_control_query: An S3 object becomes eligible today for transition and creation of a delete marker, but not permanent deletion. Which action takes precedence?
controls.negative_control_gold: Transition takes precedence over creation of a delete marker.
